articles.content:
# Business Screen (Management)

**Business management panel in Spotto**: create a business profile, manage facilities, products, trainers, and payments.

## Screen Overview

The Business screen is the management hub for your sports business. Here you'll find all the tools needed to run your business: from creating a business profile, through managing facilities and schedules, to integrating with payment and invoicing systems.

![Business screen overview: Screenshot #17](/assets/images/business/business-screen-overview.png)

### What You'll Find on the Business Screen

| Section | Description | More |
|---------|-------------|------|
| Business profile | Company data, avatar, contact details | [Details](./features/business-profile.md) |
| Sports facilities | Managing facilities and schedules | [Details](./features/facility-management.md) |
| Products | Passes, entry packages, subscriptions | [Details](./features/products.md) |
| Agents | Inviting and managing staff | [Details](./features/agent-management.md) |
| Trainers | Trainer associations, rates, permissions | [Details](./features/trainer-associations.md) |
| Child businesses | Managing branches and associated businesses | [Details](./features/child-businesses.md) |
| TPay Integration | Payment gateway configuration | [Details](./features/tpay-integration.md) |
| KSeF Integration | E-invoicing configuration | [Details](./features/ksef-integration.md) |
| Monthly reports | Revenue, reservations, statistics | [Details](./features/monthly-reports.md) |

---

## Step-by-Step Guides

### Getting Started

- [How to create a business profile](./how-to-create-profile.md): company data, tax ID, address, schedule
- [How to add a sports facility](./how-to-add-facility.md): name, type, price, availability
- [How to configure a schedule](./how-to-manage-schedule.md): opening hours, special days

### Payments and Invoicing

- [How to set up TPay](./how-to-setup-tpay.md): payment gateway for customers
- [How to set up KSeF](./how-to-setup-ksef.md): electronic invoicing

### Staff Management

- [How to manage agents](./how-to-manage-agents.md): inviting, permissions, removing
- [How to manage trainers](./how-to-manage-trainers.md): acceptance, rates, permissions

### Products and Reports

- [How to manage products](./how-to-manage-products.md): creating passes and packages
- [How to generate reports](./how-to-generate-reports.md): revenue, reservations, statistics
- [How to manage child businesses](./how-to-manage-child-businesses.md): branches, permissions

---

## Detailed Feature Descriptions

- [Business profile](./features/business-profile.md): data, avatar, contact details
- [Facility management](./features/facility-management.md): facilities, types, prices, VAT
- [Products](./features/products.md): passes, packages, validity periods
- [Agent management](./features/agent-management.md): invitations, roles
- [Trainer associations](./features/trainer-associations.md): acceptance, rates, permissions
- [Child businesses](./features/child-businesses.md): branches, TPay/tax ID permissions
- [TPay Integration](./features/tpay-integration.md): payment configuration
- [KSeF Integration](./features/ksef-integration.md): e-invoicing
- [Monthly reports](./features/monthly-reports.md): revenue analysis

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

See [Business Screen FAQ](./faq.md): answers to questions about business profiles, facilities, products, trainers, and integrations.

---

## Related Topics

- [How to book a facility (customer perspective)](../home/how-to-book-facility.md)
- [How to buy a product (customer perspective)](../shop/how-to-buy-pass.md)
- [Trainer profile](../trainer/README.md)
- [Troubleshooting payment issues](../troubleshooting/payments/payment-failed.md)

---

articles.title: FAQ: Business Screen
articles.content:
# Frequently Asked Questions: Business Screen

## Business Profile

### How can I change my company data?

Open the Business screen and tap the profile edit button. Change the necessary data and tap **Save**. [Guide](./how-to-create-profile.md)

### Can I have multiple businesses on one account?

Yes, but each business requires a separate business profile. If you have branches: use the [child businesses](./how-to-manage-child-businesses.md) system.

### How do I change the company logo?

On the Business screen, tap the camera icon on the avatar. You can take a new photo or choose one from the gallery.

---

## Sports Facilities

### How many facilities can I add?

There is no limit: you can add any number of sports facilities.

### How do I change the facility price?

Open the facility edit form, change the net or gross price (the system will automatically calculate the other value), and save. [Guide](./how-to-add-facility.md)

### Can I temporarily close a facility?

Yes: set the schedule for a specific date without any selected hours. [How to configure a schedule](./how-to-manage-schedule.md)

---

## Payments and Invoicing

### Is TPay required?

TPay is required for customers to pay online for reservations and products. Without TPay, customers will not be able to make payments in the app.

### How long does TPay activation take?

The TPay account activation process can take up to 24 hours from the moment correct data is saved.

### Is KSeF mandatory?

No: KSeF integration is optional, but recommended for invoicing automation. [How to set up KSeF](./how-to-setup-ksef.md)

---

## Agents and Staff

### How do I invite an employee?

Use the "Agents" section on the Business screen. Enter the employee's email address and send the invitation. [Guide](./how-to-manage-agents.md)

### What happens when an invitation expires?

You can resend the invitation: tap **Resend** next to the expired invitation.

### Can an agent see financial reports?

An agent has access to business management, but specific permissions depend on the configuration.

---

## Trainers

### How can a trainer start working with my business?

The trainer must send an association request from their trainer profile. The request will appear in your "Trainers" section as pending. [Guide](./how-to-manage-trainers.md)

### Can I change a trainer's rate?

Yes: tap the trainer on the list and update the hourly rate (net or gross).

### What does the "Employee" permission mean?

A trainer marked as an employee cannot independently create training sessions: they must be created by the business.

---

## Reports

### What period can I generate reports for?

You can generate reports for any month from the last 2-3 years. [Guide](./how-to-generate-reports.md)

### Can I export a report?

Yes: reports can be downloaded as PDF or shared via email.

---

## Useful Information: Feature Dependencies

Below you'll find practical information about what is required for individual features to work properly. These dependencies result from the Spotto system logic.

### When will my business appear in the search for users?

For your business to be visible in the Spotto browser and search, you must meet **four conditions**:

1. **Created business profile**: with completed data (tax ID, name, address)
2. **At least one sports facility**: added and active
3. **Configured schedule**: the facility must have at least one available time slot
4. **Profile activation by the Spotto team**: final approval after signing a cooperation agreement

| Business State | Visible in Search? | Can Accept Payments? |
|----------------|-------------------|---------------------|
| Profile only (no facilities) | No | No |
| Profile + facility (no schedule) | No | No |
| Profile + facility + schedule (no activation) | No | No |
| Profile + facility + schedule + activation | Yes | No (no TPay) |
| All of the above + TPay | Yes | Yes |

> **Tip**: Spotto **does not block** creating a profile, facilities, or TPay configuration: you can prepare everything at once. Profile activation by the Spotto team occurs after signing a cooperation agreement and is the last step before appearing in search.

### Is TPay required for the business to operate?

TPay **is not required** to create a profile, add facilities, or appear in search. However, it is **necessary to accept online payments**.

**What works WITHOUT TPay:**
- Creating a business profile and facilities
- Configuring schedules and prices
- Appearing in search (if you have facilities with a schedule)
- Managing agents and trainers
- Generating reports
- Configuring KSeF (independent of TPay)
- Creating products with the "paid at trainer" option

**What does NOT work WITHOUT TPay:**
- Accepting online payments for reservations
- Selling products with the "paid in app" option
- Processing BLIK, card, and bank transfer payments

### What are the requirements for creating products (passes, packages)?

To create a product:
1. **Business profile**: must exist
2. **Sports facility**: required if the product does not apply to all facilities (you can assign a product to selected facilities)
3. **TPay**: required **only** if the product is to be paid in the app (`Paid in app = yes`)

| Product Payment Type | Requires TPay? | Description |
|---------------------|---------------|-------------|
| **Paid in app** | Yes | Customer pays via BLIK/card/bank transfer in Spotto |
| **Paid at trainer** | No | Payment occurs outside the app |
| **Free** | No | Product without a fee |

### How do child businesses work: what is shared and what is independent?

| Element | Shared with Parent Business? | Notes |
|---------|------------------------------|-------|
| **TPay account** | Optionally | Child business can use the parent's TPay |
| **Tax ID for invoices** | Optionally | Invoices can be issued under the parent's tax ID |
| **Sports facilities** | Independent | Each business manages its own facilities |
| **Products** | Independent | Each business creates its own products |
| **Schedules** | Independent | Each business configures its own schedules |
| **Trainers** | Independent | Trainers are associated separately with each business |
| **Agents** | Independent | Each business has its own agents |
| **KSeF** | Hidden* | *If the child business uses the parent's TPay, the KSeF section is hidden: e-invoices are issued centrally |

### What are the dependencies for trainer cooperation?

- The trainer must send the association request themselves: you cannot add them manually
- After acceptance, **you set the hourly rate**: regardless of the rate the trainer set in their profile
- A trainer marked as **"Employee"** cannot independently create training sessions
- A trainer with the **"Can conduct own training sessions"** permission can see your facilities when creating classes
- A trainer can be associated with **multiple businesses** simultaneously
- Removing an association is **irreversible**: the trainer must send a new request

### Does KSeF require TPay to be configured?

**No**: KSeF and TPay are independent integrations. You can configure KSeF without TPay and vice versa. The only exception: a child business using the parent's TPay does not see the KSeF section (e-invoices are issued centrally by the parent business).

### What is the recommended order for configuring a new business?

Recommended order (from most important):

| Step | Action | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
| 1 | [Create a business profile](./how-to-create-profile.md) | Foundation: nothing works without this |
| 2 | [Add a sports facility](./how-to-add-facility.md) | Preparing your offer |
| 3 | [Configure a schedule](./how-to-manage-schedule.md) | Setting time slot availability |
| 4 | [Set up TPay](./how-to-setup-tpay.md) | Enables online payments: TPay conducts its own contractor compliance verification |
| 5 | [Add products](./how-to-manage-products.md) | Passes and packages in the Shop |
| 6 | [Set up KSeF](./how-to-setup-ksef.md) | Automatic e-invoices (optional) |
| 7 | [Invite agents](./how-to-manage-agents.md) | Delegating management (optional) |
| 8 | Activation by the Spotto team | After signing a cooperation agreement: the business appears in search |

> **Tip**: Steps 1-7 can be completed in any order, and you don't need to wait for activation. This way, when the agreement is signed, your business is ready to operate immediately.

---

## Related Topics

- [Troubleshooting payment issues](../troubleshooting/payments/payment-failed.md)
- [Trainer profile (for trainers)](../trainer/README.md)

articles.title: Agent Management
articles.content:
# Agent Management

## Description

Agents are people with access to manage your business in Spotto. They can view bookings, manage facilities, and handle customers on your behalf. The system allows you to invite new agents by email, track invitation statuses, and revoke access when needed.

## How the Invitation System Works

The entire process of adding an agent consists of four stages:

1. **Invitation**: you enter the email address of the person you want to invite and send the invitation
2. **Pending**: the invitation appears on the "Pending Invitations" list. An email with an acceptance link is sent to the provided address
3. **Acceptance**: the person clicks the link in the email and becomes an agent of your business
4. **Expiration**: if the invitation is not accepted within a specified time, it expires automatically

![Agent section: Screenshot #56](../../../assets/images/business/agent-management-section.png)

> 💡 **Tip**: You can send invitations to multiple people: each will receive a separate email.

## Active Agents

After accepting an invitation, the agent appears on the **Active Agents** list. For each agent you can see:

| Information | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Name** | Agent's data from their Spotto profile |
| **Email address** | Email used for the invitation |
| **Assignment date** | When the agent accepted the invitation |
| **Invited by** | Who sent the invitation |

### Removing an Agent

To revoke an agent's access:
1. Find the agent on the active list
2. Tap the **Remove** button
3. Confirm the removal in the dialog box

> ⚠️ **Important**: After removal, the agent immediately loses access to business management. The operation is irreversible: to restore access, you must send a new invitation.

## Pending Invitations

The pending invitations list shows people who have not yet accepted the invitation:

| Information | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Email address** | Which address the invitation was sent to |
| **Invitation date** | When the invitation was sent |
| **Invited by** | Who sent it |
| **Status** | Active or Expired |

### Available Actions

- **Resend**: sends the invitation again (useful when the email went to spam or the person missed the message)
- **Cancel**: withdraws the invitation, the link in the email stops working

> 💡 **Tip**: Expired invitations are marked with a special label. You can resend them.

## Validation

The system checks the email address validity before sending an invitation. You cannot invite:
- A person who is already an agent
- A person with an active, pending invitation
- An invalid email address

---

## Related

- [How to manage agents: step-by-step guide](../how-to-manage-agents.md)
- [Business Profile](./business-profile.md)

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-29

articles.title: Business Profile
articles.content:
# Business Profile

## Description

The business profile is your business card in the Spotto app. It contains all the data needed to run your business: from basic information, through the schedule, to payment system integration data.

## Profile Data

### Basic Data

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **NIP** | 10-digit tax identification number |
| **Company name** | Official name from the registry |
| **Display name** | Name visible to customers in the app |
| **Address** | Street, number, city, postal code |
| **Coordinates** | Location on the map (latitude/longitude) |
| **Email** | Contact email address |
| **Phone** | Contact phone number |

### Company Avatar

You can add a logo or company photo. The avatar is visible:
- On the company card in search results
- In the Business screen header
- In customers' booking details

### Schedule

The schedule defines business opening hours:
- **Weekdays**: Monday -- Friday
- **Saturday**: separate schedule
- **Sunday**: separate schedule
- **Specific dates**: schedule override for selected days

Each hourly slot (00:00 -- 23:00) can be: available, unavailable, or booked.

### Facility Floor Plan

You can upload a facility floor plan in the following formats:
- PDF
- Image (JPG, PNG)

The floor plan is visible to customers and helps with orientation within the facility.

---

## Related

- [How to create a business profile: guide](../how-to-create-profile.md)
- [How to configure the schedule](../how-to-manage-schedule.md)

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-29

articles.title: Child Businesses
articles.content:
# Child Businesses

## Description

The child business system enables managing an organizational structure with a parent company and branches. A child business can use the parent company's payment configuration (TPay) and tax data (NIP), which simplifies managing multiple locations.

## When to Use Child Businesses

Child businesses are useful when:
- You have **multiple locations** (e.g., a gym chain) and want to manage them centrally
- You want branches to **use a single TPay account**: no need to configure payments separately for each one
- You want to issue **invoices under one NIP** for the entire organization

## How the Association Works

```
Parent company (parent)
├── Child business A (Krakow branch)
├── Child business B (Warsaw branch)
└── Child business C (Wroclaw branch)
```

1. **Child business** sends an association request to the parent company
2. **Parent company** accepts or rejects the request
3. After acceptance, the parent company configures **permissions** for the branch

## Permissions

The parent company can grant the child business two independent permissions:

| Permission | Description | Effect |
|------------|-------------|--------|
| **Use parent's TPay** | Child business uses the parent company's payment gateway | Customers pay through the parent's TPay account. The child business does not need to configure its own TPay |
| **Use parent's NIP for invoices** | Invoices issued under the parent company's NIP | All invoices issued centrally under one NIP |

> ⚠️ **Important**: When a child business uses the parent's TPay, the KSeF section is automatically hidden for it: e-invoices are issued from the parent company level.

## Association Statuses

| Status | Color | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| **Pending** | Yellow | Child business sent a request, waiting for the parent company's decision |
| **Confirmed** | Green | Association active, permissions are in effect |
| **Rejected** | Red | Request was rejected by the parent company |

## Parent Company View

On the parent company's Business screen in the **Child Businesses** section you can see:

- Avatar, name, city, and email of each child business
- Association status (pending / confirmed)
- Current permissions (TPay and NIP icons)
- Action buttons: accept, reject, change permissions, remove

![Child businesses list: Screenshot #57](../../../assets/images/business/child-businesses-detail.png)

## Child Business View

If your business is a child business, on the Business screen you will see the **Parent Company Association Status** section:
- Parent company name
- Association status
- Request and confirmation dates
- Buttons: resend email, cancel request

## Changing and Removing an Association

- **Changing permissions**: the parent company can change permissions at any time (enable/disable TPay, NIP)
- **Removal**: the parent company can remove the association. The child business loses access to the parent's TPay and NIP and must configure its own

---

## Related

- [How to manage child businesses: step-by-step guide](../how-to-manage-child-businesses.md)
- [TPay Integration](./tpay-integration.md)
- [KSeF Integration](./ksef-integration.md)

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-29

articles.title: Sports Facility Management
articles.content:
# Sports Facility Management

## Description

Sports facilities are venues (courts, shooting ranges, gyms, etc.) that you make available to customers for booking. Each facility has its own name, type, price, capacity, and availability schedule.

## Facility Data

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Name** | Facility name (e.g., "Squash court #1") |
| **Type** | Facility type from a predefined list |
| **Description** | Description visible to customers |
| **Address** | Street, city, postal code |
| **Net price** | Price per hour excluding VAT |
| **VAT rate** | 0%, 5%, 8%, 23%, or EX (exempt) |
| **Gross price** | Calculated automatically (net + VAT) |
| **Min. number of slots** | Minimum booking length |
| **Max. users** | Facility capacity |
| **Price per person** | Whether the price is charged per person |

## Pricing and VAT

The system automatically converts prices:
- Enter the net price -> gross is calculated automatically
- Enter the gross price -> net is calculated automatically
- Changing the VAT rate automatically updates the other price

Available VAT rates:

| Rate | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **0%** | Zero rate |
| **5%** | Reduced rate |
| **8%** | Reduced rate (sports services) |
| **23%** | Standard rate |
| **EX** | VAT exempt |

## Facility Schedule

Each facility has its own 24-hour schedule (00:00 -- 23:00). Slots can have the following status:
- **Available**: customer can book
- **Booked**: already occupied
- **Unavailable**: facility closed

## Facility Floor Plan

You can add a facility floor plan (PDF or image) so that customers can see the venue layout.

---

## Related

- [How to add a facility: guide](../how-to-add-facility.md)
- [Business Profile](./business-profile.md)

---

articles.title: KSeF Integration
articles.content:
# KSeF Integration

## Description

KSeF (National e-Invoice System) is an electronic invoicing system operated by the Ministry of Finance. The integration allows you to automatically issue and send e-invoices directly from the Spotto app. The system supports two authorization methods: **token** and **qualified certificate**.

## Authorization Method Comparison

| Criterion | Token | Certificate |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| **Ease of setup** | Simple: copy and paste | Requires a .pfx/.p12 file and password |
| **Security level** | Standard (RSA-OAEP) | Highest (cryptographic key) |
| **Validity period** | Short: requires renewal | Long: 1-2 years (until expiration date) |
| **Cost** | Free | Depends on the certificate provider |
| **Recommended use** | Testing, quick start, small businesses | Production, larger businesses, higher requirements |
| **Source** | KSeF website of the Ministry of Finance | Qualified trust service provider |

---

## Method 1: Configuration Using a Token

An authorization token is the simplest method of connecting to KSeF. You generate it on the Ministry of Finance website and paste it into the app.

### What You Need

- KSeF authorization token (minimum 20 characters)
- You can obtain the token at: **ksef.mf.gov.pl** -> log in with a trusted profile or e-ID -> "Token generation" section

### Step by Step

**Step 1: Open the KSeF section**

On the **Business** screen, scroll to the **KSeF Status** card. If KSeF is not configured, you will see the status "Not configured" and a **Configure** button.

![KSeF status card: Screenshot #28](../../../assets/images/business/ksef-status-card.png)

**Step 2: Tap "Configure"**

The KSeF configuration screen will open with a choice of authorization method.

**Step 3: Select the "Token" tab**

On the configuration screen, tap the **Token** tab (it should be active by default).

**Step 4: Paste the authorization token**

In the **KSeF Token** field, paste the token copied from the KSeF Ministry of Finance website.

| Validation | Requirement |
|------------|-------------|
| **Minimum length** | 20 characters |
| **Allowed characters** | Letters, digits, special characters |
| **Copying** | Copy/paste recommended: avoid manual retyping |

> 💡 **Tip**: You can copy the token from the KSeF website and paste it in the app. Make sure you copied the entire token without truncation.

**Step 5: Select the environment**

Choose the KSeF working environment:

| Environment | API Address | Description |
|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| **Test** | ksef-test.mf.gov.pl | For testing: invoices are not official, they do not enter the KSeF system |
| **Production** | ksef.mf.gov.pl | Real e-invoices sent to the official KSeF system |

> 💡 **Tip**: We recommend starting with the **test** environment to make sure the configuration works correctly, and only then switching to production.

![KSeF token configuration: Screenshot #29](../../../assets/images/business/ksef-config-token.png)

**Step 6: Tap "Save"**

The system will automatically:
1. Encrypt the token with the KSeF public key (RSA-OAEP)
2. Test the connection to the KSeF server in the selected environment
3. Verify permissions to issue invoices for your NIP
4. Display the test result

**Test result:**
- ✅ **Green icon**: connection works correctly, KSeF is ready
- ❌ **Red icon**: connection error, check the token and try again

---

## Method 2: Configuration Using a Qualified Certificate

A qualified certificate provides the highest level of security. It is the same technology as the qualified signature used in e-government and electronic banking.

### What You Need

- A qualified certificate file in **PKCS#12** format (extension .pfx or .p12)
- **Password** for the certificate file (set during export)
- The certificate must come from a qualified trust service provider

| Provider | Website |
|----------|---------|
| **Certum** | certum.pl |
| **KIR** | elektronicznypodpis.pl |
| **Sigillum** | sigillum.pl |
| **PWPW** | sigillum.pl |
| **EuroCert** | eurocert.pl |

### Step by Step

**Step 1: Open the KSeF section**

On the **Business** screen, scroll to the **KSeF Status** card and tap **Configure**. If KSeF was previously configured with a token, tap **Reconfigure**.

**Step 2: Select the "Certificate" tab**

On the configuration screen, tap the **Certificate** tab.

![KSeF certificate configuration: Screenshot #55](../../../assets/images/business/ksef-config-certificate.png)

**Step 3: Upload the certificate file**

Tap the **Select certificate file** button. The system file picker will open. Point to the certificate file on your device.

| Supported format | Extension | Description |
|------------------|-----------|-------------|
| **PKCS#12** | .pfx | Microsoft format: contains private key and certificate |
| **PKCS#12** | .p12 | Standard format: identical to .pfx |

After selecting the file, you will see its name displayed below the button.

> ⚠️ **Important**: Make sure the certificate file comes from a qualified trust service provider. Self-signed certificates are not accepted by KSeF.

**Step 4: Enter the certificate password**

In the **Certificate password** field, enter the password protecting the .pfx/.p12 file.

| Important information about the password | |
|------------------------------------------|---|
| **What is this password?** | The password set during export of the certificate to a .pfx/.p12 file |
| **Is it stored?** | No: the password is used once to read the private key |
| **I don't remember the password** | Re-export the certificate with a new password from your provider |

> 💡 **Tip**: This password is for the certificate file itself, **not** the password for your account with the certificate provider. These are two different passwords.

**Step 5: Select the environment**

Same as with the token method: choose **Test** or **Production**.

| Environment | API Address | Description |
|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| **Test** | ksef-test.mf.gov.pl | For testing: invoices do not enter the official system |
| **Production** | ksef.mf.gov.pl | Real e-invoices in the KSeF system |

**Step 6: Tap "Save"**

The system will automatically:
1. Read the certificate from the file using the provided password
2. Verify the certificate validity (start and expiration dates)
3. Check whether the certificate comes from a qualified provider
4. Use the private key to sign the authorization request
5. Test the connection to the KSeF server in the selected environment
6. Verify permissions to issue invoices for your NIP
7. Display the test result

**Test result:**
- ✅ **Green icon**: certificate valid, connection works
- ❌ **Red icon**: error, check the certificate, password, and expiration date

---

## Security

### Token: How the Security Works

After entering the token, the app:
1. Encrypts the token with the KSeF public key using the **RSA-OAEP** algorithm
2. Sends the encrypted token to the KSeF server
3. KSeF decrypts the token with its private key and opens a session
4. The session is additionally secured with a symmetric **AES-256** key

The token **is not stored** locally on the device after saving the configuration.

### Certificate: How the Security Works

The qualified certificate contains a private key that:
1. Is used to **cryptographically sign** authorization requests
2. Provides **non-repudiation**: only the certificate holder could have signed the request
3. Is protected by the .pfx/.p12 file password

The certificate password **is not stored**: it is used once to read the key.

---

## Status and Management

After configuring KSeF (with either method), the KSeF card will show:

| Element | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Status** | Configured / Not configured |
| **Method** | Token or Certificate |
| **Environment** | Label: Test or Production |
| **Last sync** | Date and time of last communication with KSeF |
| **Toggle** | Enable/disable KSeF integration without removing the configuration |
| **Reconfigure** | Button to change settings or authorization method |

### Changing the Authorization Method

You can change the method at any time:
1. Tap **Reconfigure** on the KSeF card
2. Select the new tab (Token -> Certificate or vice versa)
3. Enter the new authorization data
4. Save: the new configuration will replace the previous one

### Disabling the Integration

The toggle on the KSeF card allows you to temporarily disable the integration without removing the configuration. After re-enabling, you do not need to re-enter the data.

---

## Limitations

| Limitation | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| **Child businesses** | KSeF is not available for child businesses using the parent company's TPay: e-invoices are issued centrally |
| **Token validity** | The token has a limited validity period: after expiration, generate a new one on the KSeF website |
| **Certificate validity** | After the certificate expires, you must obtain a new one from the provider and reconfigure |
| **Qualified providers** | Only certificates from qualified trust service providers are accepted |

---

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Token connection test failed | Token expired or invalid | Generate a new token on the KSeF Ministry of Finance website |
| "Token appears to be invalid" | Token too short | Token must be at least 20 characters: copy the entire token |
| Certificate not accepted | Wrong file format | Use .pfx or .p12 format (PKCS#12) |
| Certificate password error | Wrong file password | This is the certificate file password, not the provider account password |
| Certificate expired | Validity date has passed | Obtain a new certificate from the provider |
| KSeF section not visible | Child business with parent's TPay | KSeF is managed by the parent company |
| Server connection error | KSeF server unavailable | Try again in a few minutes: the KSeF server may be undergoing maintenance |

---

## Related

- [How to configure KSeF: guide](../how-to-setup-ksef.md)
- [TPay Integration](./tpay-integration.md)
- [Child Businesses](./child-businesses.md)

---

articles.title: Monthly Reports
articles.content:
# Monthly Reports

## Description

Monthly reports give you a complete picture of your business's financial and operational performance for a selected month. You can analyze revenue, number of bookings, cancellations, and a detailed breakdown by individual sports facilities.

## Report Types

### Summary Report

A quick overview of key metrics for the selected month:

| Metric | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Total revenue** | Sum of revenue from all facilities (in PLN) |
| **Total number of bookings** | How many bookings were completed |
| **Number of cancellations** | How many bookings were cancelled |
| **Facility breakdown** | Detailed data for each facility |

### Detailed Report

Contains everything from the summary, plus detailed lists:

| Section | Content |
|---------|---------|
| **Bookings** | List of individual bookings with dates, participants, and amounts |
| **Product purchases** | Pass and package purchase transactions with prices |
| **Trainer sessions** | List of trainer sessions with hours and rates |

![Monthly report: Screenshot #60](../../../assets/images/business/monthly-report-detail.png)

## Facility Breakdown

In both report types you will find a table with the breakdown by individual facilities:

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Facility name** | Which sports facility |
| **Revenue** | Total revenue from this facility (PLN) |
| **Bookings** | Number of completed bookings |
| **Cancellations** | Number of cancelled bookings |

This allows you to see which facilities generate the most revenue and where the most cancellations occur.

## Period Selection

You can generate a report for any month:
- **Month**: select from the list (January -- December)
- **Year**: select from the available years (last 2-3 years)

> 💡 **Tip**: Generate reports regularly (e.g., at the end of each month) to track trends and make better business decisions.

## Export and Sharing

You can do the following with a generated report:
- **Download as PDF**: save to your device for archiving or accounting
- **Share**: send by email, messenger, or another app
- **Print**: print directly from your device

## Usage Examples

| Use Case | Which Report |
|----------|--------------|
| Quick monthly analysis | Summary |
| Accounting settlement | Detailed (PDF) |
| Facility comparison | Summary (breakdown table) |
| Trainer settlement | Detailed (trainer sessions) |
| Cancellation analysis | Summary (cancellation metric) |

---

## Related

- [How to generate reports: step-by-step guide](../how-to-generate-reports.md)
- [Facility Management](./facility-management.md)
- [Trainer Associations](./trainer-associations.md)

articles.content:
# Products (Passes and Packages)

## Description

Products are offers that you create for customers in the Spotto Shop. They can be passes, entry packages, or subscriptions with various prices, number of uses, and validity periods.

## Product Properties

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Title** | Name visible in the Shop |
| **Subtitle** | Additional description (optional) |
| **Description** | Detailed product description |
| **Net/gross price** | With automatic VAT conversion |
| **Number of uses** | How many times the customer can use it |
| **Period** | Days, Weeks, Months, Years, Lifetime |
| **Number of periods** | E.g., "3" months |
| **Active** | Whether the product is visible in the Shop |

## Payment Options

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Payable in app** | Customer uses the product to pay for a booking |
| **Payable at trainer** | Customer uses the product to pay for a training session |

## Facility Assignment

A product can be valid:
- **At all facilities** of the business
- **At selected facilities**: you select specific locations

## Time Restriction

Optionally, you can set:
- **Start date**: product available in the Shop from this date
- **End date**: product disappears from the Shop after this date

---

## Related

- [How to manage products: guide](../how-to-manage-products.md)
- [Product catalog (customer perspective)](../../shop/features/product-catalog.md)

articles.title: TPay Integration
articles.content:
# TPay Integration

## Description

TPay is a certified payment gateway that enables accepting online payments from customers. TPay configuration is required for customers to be able to pay for sports facility bookings and products (passes, packages) in the Spotto app.

## Why TPay Is Important

Without TPay configured:
- Customers **cannot pay** for bookings in the app
- Your products **will not be available** for purchase in the Shop
- Trainers **cannot collect fees** for training sessions

## Required Data

TPay configuration requires providing company registration data:

| Field | Format | Description |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| **NIP** | 10 digits | Company tax identification number |
| **Email** | address@company.pl | Company contact email |
| **Phone** | phone number | Company contact phone number |
| **REGON** | 9 or 14 digits | REGON number from the GUS registry |
| **KRS** | 10 digits | National Court Register number |
| **Legal form** | from dropdown list | Legal entity type (e.g., LLC, joint-stock company, sole proprietorship) |
| **Category (MCC)** | from dropdown list | Merchant Category Code |
| **Website** | URL | Website address (must start with http:// or https://) |
| **Contact person first name** | text | Company representative's first name |
| **Contact person last name** | text | Company representative's last name |

![TPay configuration form: Screenshot #58](../../../assets/images/business/tpay-config-detail.png)

> ⚠️ **Important**: All fields are required. Incorrect data will result in registration rejection.

## Data Validation

The system automatically checks data correctness:
- **NIP**: exactly 10 digits
- **REGON**: 9 or 14 digits
- **KRS**: exactly 10 digits
- **Email**: valid address format
- **URL**: must start with http:// or https://

Validation errors are displayed next to the corresponding form fields.

## Automatic Registration

After enabling the **"Automatic TPay registration"** option, the system:
1. Sends company data to TPay
2. Creates a merchant account
3. Assigns a merchant ID (Merchant ID) to your profile

The activation process may take up to **24 hours**.

## Payment Methods for Customers

After TPay configuration, your customers can pay using:

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Payment card** | Visa, Mastercard, and others |
| **Instant transfer** | Online transfer from the customer's bank |
| **BLIK** | BLIK code from a banking app |

## Child Businesses and TPay

Child businesses can use your TPay configuration: they do not need to configure their own account. This simplifies payment management in organizations with multiple locations. [More about child businesses](./child-businesses.md)

## Security

- TPay is a certified payment gateway compliant with PCI DSS
- Spotto **does not store** customer card data
- All transactions are **encrypted**
- Company registration data is transmitted through a secure channel

---

## Related

- [How to configure TPay: step-by-step guide](../how-to-setup-tpay.md)
- [Payments (customer perspective)](../../shop/features/payments.md)
- [Child Businesses](./child-businesses.md)

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-29

articles.title: Trainer Associations
articles.content:
# Trainer Associations

## Description

The trainer association system allows you to manage collaboration with trainers who conduct sessions at your sports facilities. You can accept or reject trainer requests, set hourly rates, grant permissions, and control what trainers can do at your facilities.

## How Collaboration Works

1. **The trainer searches for your business** in the app and sends a collaboration request
2. **You receive the request** in the "Trainers" section on the Business screen
3. **You configure the rate and permissions** before accepting
4. **You accept**: the trainer can conduct sessions at your facilities

## Association Statuses

| Status | Color | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| **Pending** | Yellow/orange | Trainer sent a request: waiting for your decision |
| **Confirmed** | Green | Collaboration active: trainer conducts sessions |
| **Rejected** | Red | Request rejected |

![Trainer associations list: Screenshot #59](../../../assets/images/business/trainer-associations-detail.png)

## Hourly Rates

For each trainer, you set an individual hourly rate:

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Net rate** | Price per hour excluding VAT (in PLN) |
| **VAT rate** | Choose: 0%, 8%, or 23% |
| **Gross rate** | Calculated automatically based on net + VAT |

The system supports **bidirectional conversion**:
- Enter net -> gross is calculated automatically
- Enter gross -> net is calculated automatically
- Change VAT -> the other value is automatically updated

> 💡 **Tip**: The rate set by you is independent of the rate the trainer has set in their profile. You decide how much you pay the trainer per hour.

## Trainer Permissions

For each confirmed trainer, you can set three permissions:

| Permission | Description | Default |
|------------|-------------|---------|
| **Can conduct own trainings** | Trainer independently creates and manages trainings at your facilities | Disabled |
| **Employee** | Trainer is your full-time employee (cannot independently create trainings) | Disabled |
| **Max. number of participants** | Limit of people per session conducted by this trainer | No limit |

> ⚠️ **Important**: A trainer marked as "Employee" **cannot** independently create trainings: they must be created on the business side.

## Trainer Information

For each associated trainer you can see:
- **Avatar and name**: profile photo and trainer's name
- **Email address**: contact details
- **Request date**: when the trainer sent the collaboration request
- **Confirmation date**: when you accepted the request
- **Current rates**: net, VAT, gross
- **Current permissions**: labels on the trainer's profile

## Managing Existing Associations

For confirmed trainers, you can at any time:
- **Change the rate**: update the hourly price (net or gross)
- **Change permissions**: adjust what the trainer can do
- **Remove the association**: end collaboration with the trainer

> ⚠️ **Important**: Removing an association is irreversible. The trainer loses access to your facilities. To resume collaboration, the trainer must send a new request.

---

## Related

- [How to manage trainers: step-by-step guide](../how-to-manage-trainers.md)
- [Trainer Profile (for trainers)](../../trainer/README.md)
- [Agent Management](./agent-management.md)

---

articles.title: How to Add a Sports Facility
articles.content:
# How to Add a Sports Facility

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Medium

## Overview

Sports facilities are the places you offer customers for booking: courts, shooting ranges, gyms, etc. Each facility has its own name, type, price, and availability schedule.

---

## Before You Start

- You must have a [business profile created](./how-to-create-profile.md)

---

## Steps

### Step 1: Open the "Facilities" Section

On the Business screen, find the **Sports Facilities** section and tap the **Add Facility** button.

![Facilities section: Screenshot #21](/assets/images/business/facilities-section.png)

### Step 2: Fill in Facility Data

Complete the facility form:

| Field | Description | Required |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| **Name** | Facility name (e.g., "Squash Court #1") | Yes |
| **Type** | Facility type (from dropdown list) | Yes |
| **Description** | Facility description for customers | No |
| **Address** | Street and number of the facility | Yes |
| **City** | City | Yes |
| **Postal code** | Postal code | Yes |

![Facility form: Screenshot #22](/assets/images/business/facility-form.png)

### Step 3: Set the Price

Configure the hourly rate:
- **Net price**: price without VAT
- **VAT rate**: choose from the list: 0%, 5%, 8%, 23%, or EX (exempt)
- **Gross price**: calculated automatically based on the net price and VAT rate

> **Tip**: You can also enter the gross price: the net price will be calculated automatically.

![Price configuration: Screenshot #23](/assets/images/business/facility-pricing.png)

### Step 4: Set Additional Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Minimum number of slots** | Minimum booking duration (e.g., 1 hour) |
| **Maximum number of users** | Facility capacity (how many people at once) |
| **Price per person** | Whether the price is charged per person (toggle) |

### Step 5: Configure Availability

Select the hours during which the facility is available for booking:
- Choose hourly slots (from 00:00 to 23:00)
- Selected = available, deselected = unavailable

### Step 6: (Optional) Upload a Facility Plan

You can add a facility plan in PDF format or as a photo so customers can see the layout.

### Step 7: Save the Facility

Tap **Save**. The facility will appear on the list in the "Sports Facilities" section.

---

## Verification

The facility was added correctly when:
- You can see it on the facilities list on the Business screen
- Customers can find it in the search on the Home screen

---

## Troubleshooting

### Problem: I can't save the facility
**Solution**: Check that all required fields (name, type, address) are filled in and that the price is a valid number.

---

## Related Topics

- [How to configure a schedule](./how-to-manage-schedule.md)
- [Facility management: details](./features/facility-management.md)
- [How to create a business profile](./how-to-create-profile.md)

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-29

articles.title: How to Create a Business Profile
articles.content:
# How to Create a Business Profile

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Medium

## Overview

The business profile is your business card in Spotto. It contains company data, address, opening hours, and contact information. Creating a profile is the first step to accepting reservations from customers.

---

## Before You Start

- You must have a Spotto account with an active "Business" role (you can enable it in [profile settings](../profile/how-to-manage-roles.md))
- Prepare your company data: tax ID (NIP), REGON, address, contact details
- If you plan to accept online payments: also prepare data for [TPay configuration](./how-to-setup-tpay.md)

---

## Steps

### Step 1: Open the Business Screen

On the bottom navigation bar, tap the **Business** icon. If you don't have a business profile yet, you'll see the profile creation form.

![Business profile creation screen: Screenshot #18](/assets/images/business/create-profile-form.png)

### Step 2: Fill in Basic Data

Complete the required fields:

| Field | Description | Required |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| **Tax ID (NIP)** | Company tax identification number | Yes |
| **Company name** | Official name from the registry | Yes |
| **Display name** | Name visible to customers | Yes |
| **Address** | Street and number | Yes |
| **City** | City of headquarters | Yes |
| **Postal code** | Postal code (XX-XXX) | Yes |
| **Email** | Contact email address | Yes |
| **Phone** | Contact phone number | Yes |

![Basic data form: Screenshot #19](/assets/images/business/profile-basic-info.png)

### Step 3: Add a Company Avatar

Tap the camera icon on the profile picture to add a logo or company photo:
- **Take a photo**: use the camera
- **Choose from gallery**: select an existing photo

### Step 4: Set the Schedule

Configure opening hours:
- **Weekdays**: availability hours from Monday to Friday
- **Saturday**: Saturday hours
- **Sunday**: Sunday hours

Each hour (from 00:00 to 23:00) can be marked as available or unavailable.

![Schedule configuration: Screenshot #20](/assets/images/business/schedule-config.png)

> **Tip**: You can also set special hours for specific dates (e.g., holidays). [How to configure a schedule](./how-to-manage-schedule.md)

### Step 5: Set Location Coordinates

Tap the coordinates field to open the map and indicate the exact location of your business. This will help customers find your facility on the map.

### Step 6: (Optional) Upload a Facility Plan

You can upload a facility plan in PDF format or as a photo. The plan will be visible to customers.

### Step 7: Save the Profile

Tap the **Save** button at the bottom of the form. Your business profile will be created.

---

## Verification

The profile was created correctly when:
- You see the Business screen with the company name and avatar in the header
- Company data is visible in the profile section

---

## What's Next?

After creating the profile:
1. [Add a sports facility](./how-to-add-facility.md): create your first facility
2. [Set up TPay](./how-to-setup-tpay.md): enable online payments
3. [Create products](./how-to-manage-products.md): add passes and packages

---

## Troubleshooting

### Problem: I can't save the profile
**Solution**: Check that all required fields are filled in. The tax ID (NIP) must have exactly 10 digits.

### Problem: I don't see the Business tab
**Solution**: Make sure you have the "Business" role active. Go to [Profile -> Profile Types](../profile/how-to-manage-roles.md) and enable the business role.

---

## Related Topics

- [How to add a sports facility](./how-to-add-facility.md)
- [How to set up TPay](./how-to-setup-tpay.md)
- [Business profile: details](./features/business-profile.md)

---

articles.content:
# How to Manage Agents

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Easy

## Overview

Agents are people who help you manage your business in Spotto. You can invite employees, grant them access to the management panel, and remove them from the team if needed.

---

## Inviting an Agent

### Step 1: Open the "Agents" Section

On the Business screen, find the **Agents** section and tap **Invite Agent**.

### Step 2: Enter the Email Address

Enter the email address of the person you want to invite. The system will send an invitation to the provided address.

![Inviting an agent: Screenshot #30](/assets/images/business/invite-agent.png)

### Step 3: Wait for Acceptance

The invitation will appear on the **Pending Invitations** list with information:
- Email address of the invited person
- Invitation date
- Who sent the invitation
- Validity status (invitations expire after a specified time)

> **Tip**: If an invitation has expired, you can resend it using the **Resend** button.

---

## Managing Active Agents

On the **Active Agents** list, you can see:
- Agent's full name
- Email address
- Assignment date
- Who assigned the agent

### Removing an Agent

1. Find the agent on the list
2. Tap the **Remove** button
3. Confirm the removal

> **Important**: After removal, the agent immediately loses access to managing your business.

---

## Managing Invitations

On the **Pending Invitations** list, you can:
- **Resend**: if the person didn't receive the invitation
- **Cancel**: if you want to withdraw the invitation

Expired invitations are marked with a special label.

---

## Related Topics

- [Agent management: details](./features/agent-management.md)
- [How to create a business profile](./how-to-create-profile.md)

---

articles.title: How to Manage Child Businesses
articles.content:
# How to Manage Child Businesses

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Advanced

## Overview

The child business system allows parent companies to manage branches and associated businesses. A child business can use the parent business's TPay configuration and tax ID (NIP).

---

## Managing from the Parent Business

### Viewing Child Businesses

In the **Child Businesses** section on the Business screen, you'll see:
- **Pending**: new association requests
- **Confirmed**: active child businesses

Each child business displays: avatar, name, city, and email.

![Child businesses list: Screenshot #37](/assets/images/business/child-businesses-list.png)

### Accepting a Child Business

1. Find the pending request
2. Configure permissions:

| Permission | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| **Use parent's TPay** | Child business uses the parent business's payment gateway |
| **Use parent's tax ID for invoices** | Invoices issued under the parent business's tax ID |

3. Tap **Accept** or **Reject**

### Changing Permissions

For confirmed child businesses, you can change permissions (TPay, tax ID) at any time and save the changes.

### Removing an Association

Tap **Remove** next to the child business and confirm. The child business will lose access to the parent business's TPay and tax ID.

---

## From the Child Business Perspective

If your business is a child business:
- On the Business screen, you'll see a **Parent Business Association Status** section
- The status can be: pending, confirmed, or rejected

articles.content:
# How to Manage Products

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Medium

## Overview

Products are passes, entry packages, and subscriptions that you offer customers in the Spotto Shop. You can create products with different prices, number of entries, and validity periods.

---

## Creating a Product

### Step 1: Open the "Products" Section

On the Business screen, find the **Products** section and tap **Add Product**.

### Step 2: Fill in Product Data

| Field | Description | Required |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| **Title** | Product name (e.g., "Monthly Pass") | Yes |
| **Subtitle** | Additional description | No |
| **Description** | Detailed product description | Yes |
| **Net price** | Price without VAT (in PLN) | Yes |
| **VAT rate** | 0%, 5%, 8%, 23%, or EX | Yes |
| **Gross price** | Calculated automatically | : |
| **Number of uses** | How many times the customer can use it | Yes |
| **Period** | Days, Weeks, Months, Years, or Lifetime | Yes |
| **Number of periods** | E.g., "3" months | Yes |

![Product form: Screenshot #34](/assets/images/business/product-form.png)

### Step 3: Set Payment Options

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Paid in app** | Customer can use the product to pay for a reservation |
| **Paid at trainer** | Customer can use the product to pay for a training session |

### Step 4: Assign to Facilities

Choose which facilities the product is valid for:
- **All facilities**: pass valid at all your locations
- **Selected facilities**: select specific facilities from the list

### Step 5: (Optional) Set Dates

You can limit product availability over time:
- **Start date**: from when the product is available in the Shop
- **End date**: until when the product is available in the Shop

### Step 6: Save the Product

Tap **Save**. The product will appear on your list and in the Shop for customers.

---

## Editing and Deleting Products

- **Edit**: tap the product on the list to open the edit form
- **Delete**: tap the delete icon and confirm

> **Important**: Deleting a product does not affect already purchased copies: customers can still use them until they expire.

---

## Related Topics

- [Products: details](./features/products.md)
- [How to buy a product (customer perspective)](../shop/how-to-buy-pass.md)
- [How to add a sports facility](./how-to-add-facility.md)

articles.content:
# How to Configure a Schedule

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Easy

## Overview

The schedule defines when your facilities are available for booking. You can set a general weekly schedule and override it for specific dates (e.g., holidays, special events).

---

## Steps

### Step 1: Open the Schedule

On the Business screen, go to the business profile or facility edit view, then find the **Schedule** section.

### Step 2: Set the Weekly Schedule

Configure opening hours for three day types:

| Day Type | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Weekdays** | Monday - Friday |
| **Saturday** | Saturday |
| **Sunday** | Sunday |

For each day type, select the hours (slots from 00:00 to 23:00) when the facility is available.

![Weekly schedule: Screenshot #24](/assets/images/business/weekly-schedule.png)

> **Tip**: Selected hour = facility available. Deselected = closed.

### Step 3: (Optional) Add Availability for a Specific Date

To override the weekly schedule for a specific date:

1. Select the **Schedule for a specific date** mode
2. Choose a date from the calendar
3. Select availability hours for that day

The date-specific schedule takes higher priority than the weekly schedule.

![Date-specific availability: Screenshot #25](/assets/images/business/date-specific-schedule.png)

### Step 4: Save Changes

Tap **Save** to confirm the new schedule.

---

## Usage Examples

| Situation | Solution |
|-----------|----------|
| Holiday closure | Add a specific date with no selected hours |
| Extended weekend hours | Set more slots for Saturday |
| One-time event | Add a date with custom hours |

---

## Related Topics

articles.content:
# How to Manage Trainers

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Medium

## Overview

Trainers can conduct classes at your sports facilities. When a trainer sends a cooperation request, you'll see it on the Business screen. You can accept it, set an hourly rate, and grant permissions.

---

## Accepting a Trainer

### Step 1: Check Pending Requests

On the Business screen, in the **Trainers** section, you'll see a list of associations with statuses:
- **Pending** (yellow): new requests to review
- **Confirmed** (green): accepted trainers

![Trainer list: Screenshot #31](/assets/images/business/trainer-associations-list.png)

### Step 2: Set the Hourly Rate

Before or after acceptance, tap the trainer to open the rate panel:
- **Net rate**: hourly rate without VAT
- **VAT rate**: choose: 0%, 8%, or 23%
- **Gross rate**: calculated automatically

![Setting trainer rate: Screenshot #32](/assets/images/business/trainer-pricing.png)

> **Tip**: You can also enter the gross rate: the net rate will be calculated automatically.

### Step 3: Set Permissions

Configure what the trainer can do at your facility:

| Permission | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| **Can conduct own training sessions** | Trainer independently creates and manages training sessions |
| **Employee** | Trainer is your full-time employee |
| **Max number of participants** | Participant limit per training session |

![Trainer permissions: Screenshot #33](/assets/images/business/trainer-permissions.png)

### Step 4: Accept or Reject

- **Accept**: the trainer is added to your team
- **Reject**: the trainer will be notified of the rejection

---

## Managing Existing Trainers

For confirmed trainers, you can:
- **Change the rate**: update the hourly price
- **Change permissions**: adjust what the trainer can do
- **Remove association**: end the cooperation with the trainer

---

articles.title: How to Set Up KSeF
articles.content:
# How to Set Up KSeF

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Advanced

## Overview

KSeF (Krajowy System e-Faktur / National e-Invoice System) is an electronic invoicing system operated by the Ministry of Finance. Integration with KSeF enables automatic issuance and submission of e-invoices.

Spotto supports two KSeF authorization methods:
- **Authorization token**: quick setup, ideal for getting started
- **Qualified certificate**: higher security level, recommended for production use

---

## Before You Start

- You must have a [business profile created](./how-to-create-profile.md)
- Choose an authorization method and prepare the appropriate data (see table below)
- Decide whether you want to test the connection in a test environment first

| Method | What You Need | Where to Obtain |
|--------|--------------|-----------------|
| **Token** | Authorization token (min. 20 characters) | KSeF website of the Ministry of Finance |
| **Certificate** | Certificate file (.pfx or .p12) + password | Qualified trust service provider |

> **Important**: KSeF is not available for child businesses using the parent business's TPay.

---

## Method 1: Configuration Using a Token

The authorization token is the simplest method to connect with KSeF. You generate it on the Ministry of Finance website and paste it in the app.

### Step 1: Find the KSeF Section

On the Business screen, find the **KSeF Status** card. If KSeF is not configured, you'll see the message "Not configured."

![KSeF status card: Screenshot #28](/assets/images/business/ksef-status-card.png)

### Step 2: Open the Configuration

Tap the **Configure** button on the KSeF card. The configuration window will open.

### Step 3: Select the "Token" Method

In the configuration window, select the **Token** tab or option.

### Step 4: Enter the Token

In the **KSeF Token** field, paste the authorization token obtained from the KSeF system.

> **Tip**: You can copy the token from the KSeF website and paste it in the app. The data is secure: the token is not stored locally after saving. The token must be at least 20 characters long.

### Step 5: Choose the Environment

Select the working environment:

| Environment | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Test** | Testing environment: invoices are not actually sent |
| **Production** | Real e-invoices sent to the KSeF system |

> **Tip**: We recommend starting with the test environment to make sure everything works correctly.

![KSeF token configuration: Screenshot #29](/assets/images/business/ksef-config-token.png)

### Step 6: Save and Test

Tap **Save**. The system will automatically test the connection with KSeF and display the result:
- **Green icon**: connection works correctly
- **Red icon**: check the token and try again

---

## Method 2: Configuration Using a Certificate

A qualified certificate provides a higher level of security and is recommended for long-term production use. Unlike a token, a certificate does not require regular renewal (until the certificate expires).

### Step 1: Find the KSeF Section

On the Business screen, find the **KSeF Status** card and tap **Configure**.

### Step 2: Select the "Certificate" Method

In the configuration window, select the **Certificate** tab or option.

![KSeF certificate configuration: Screenshot #55](/assets/images/business/ksef-config-certificate.png)

### Step 3: Upload the Certificate File

Tap the **Select certificate file** button and point to the certificate file on your device.

Supported formats:

| Format | Extension | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| **PKCS#12** | .pfx, .p12 | Most commonly used format, contains private key and certificate |

> **Important**: Make sure the certificate file comes from a qualified trust service provider (e.g., Certum, KIR, Sigillum, PWPW, EuroCert).

### Step 4: Enter the Certificate Password

Enter the password protecting the certificate file. This is the password that was set during certificate export.

> **Tip**: The password is used once to read the certificate and is not stored in the app.

### Step 5: Choose the Environment

Same as with the token method: select **Test** or **Production**.

### Step 6: Save and Test

Tap **Save**. The system will:
1. Read the certificate from the file
2. Verify its validity
3. Test the connection with KSeF
4. Display the test result

---

## Which Method Is Better?

| Criterion | Token | Certificate |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| **Ease of setup** | Simpler: copy and paste | Requires a certificate file |
| **Security** | Standard | Higher: cryptographic key |
| **Validity** | Limited: requires renewal | Valid until the certificate expiration date |
| **Recommended use** | Testing, small businesses | Production, larger businesses |
| **Requirements** | Account on the KSeF website | Qualified certificate |

> **Tip**: You can start with a token in the test environment and later switch to a certificate in production.

---

## Managing KSeF

After configuration (regardless of the chosen method), the KSeF card will show:
- **Configuration status**: Configured / Not configured
- **Authorization method**: Token or Certificate
- **Environment**: Test or Production (label)
- **Last synchronization**: date and time
- **Toggle**: enable/disable KSeF integration

### Changing the Authorization Method

To change the method from token to certificate (or vice versa):
1. Tap **Reconfigure** on the KSeF card
2. Select the new method
3. Enter the appropriate data
4. Save: the new configuration will replace the previous one

---

## Troubleshooting

### Problem: Token connection test failed
**Solution**: Check that the token is valid and current. KSeF tokens have an expiration date: generate a new one on the Ministry of Finance KSeF website.

### Problem: Error "Token appears to be invalid"
**Solution**: The token must be at least 20 characters long. Make sure you copied the entire token without truncation.

### Problem: Certificate is not accepted
**Solution**:
1. Check that the file is in .pfx or .p12 format
2. Make sure the password is correct
3. Check the certificate expiration date: an expired certificate will not be accepted
4. The certificate must come from a qualified trust service provider

### Problem: Certificate password error
**Solution**: The password refers to the certificate file (set during export), not the account password at the provider. If you don't remember the password, export the certificate again with a new password.

### Problem: I don't see the KSeF section
**Solution**: The KSeF section is hidden if your business is a child business using the parent business's TPay.

---

## Related Topics

- [KSeF Integration: details](./features/ksef-integration.md)
- [How to set up TPay](./how-to-setup-tpay.md)

---

articles.title: How to Set Up TPay
articles.content:
# How to Set Up TPay

**Screen**: Business
**Role**: Business Owner
**Difficulty**: Advanced

## Overview

TPay is a payment gateway that enables your customers to pay for reservations and products online. TPay configuration requires providing your company's registration data.

---

## Before You Start

- You must have a [business profile created](./how-to-create-profile.md)
- Prepare the data: tax ID (NIP), REGON, KRS, website address
- Contact person details (first name, last name)

---

## Steps

### Step 1: Open Business Profile Edit

On the Business screen, tap the profile edit button. Scroll to the **TPay Data** section.

![TPay section: Screenshot #26](/assets/images/business/tpay-section.png)

### Step 2: Fill in the Required Data

| Field | Description | Required |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| **Tax ID (NIP)** | Tax identification number | Yes |
| **Email** | Company email | Yes |
| **Phone** | Contact number | Yes |
| **REGON** | REGON number (9 or 14 digits) | Yes |
| **KRS** | KRS number (10 digits) | Yes |
| **Legal form** | Choose from the dropdown list | Yes |
| **Category (MCC)** | Business activity category code | Yes |
| **Website** | URL address (http:// or https://) | Yes |
| **Contact person first name** | Representative's first name | Yes |
| **Contact person last name** | Representative's last name | Yes |

![TPay data form: Screenshot #27](/assets/images/business/tpay-form.png)

### Step 3: Enable Automatic Registration

Toggle the **Automatic TPay registration** option: the system will automatically create a merchant account based on the provided data.

### Step 4: Save the Profile

Tap **Save**. The data will be verified and submitted to the TPay system.

---

## Verification

TPay configuration is correct when:
- Customers can pay for reservations at your facilities
- Customers can purchase your products in the Shop

---

## Troubleshooting

### Problem: I can't save TPay data
**Solution**: Check the formats:
- NIP: exactly 10 digits
- REGON: 9 or 14 digits
- KRS: 10 digits
- URL: must start with http:// or https://

### Problem: Customers can't pay
**Solution**: Make sure all TPay fields are filled in and the profile is saved. The TPay account activation process can take up to 24 hours.

---

## Related Topics

- [TPay Integration: details](./features/tpay-integration.md)
- [How to set up KSeF](./how-to-setup-ksef.md)
- [Shop payments](../shop/features/payments.md)

articles.title: FAQ : Home Screen
articles.content:
# Frequently Asked Questions : Home Screen

## Searching for facilities

### How can I find a sports facility near me?

You have two options:
1. **Search bar** : type the name of a city in the search bar at the top of the Home screen and select a date. [Step-by-step guide](./how-to-search-facilities.md)
2. **Map** : open the map view, which will automatically show facilities near your location. [How to use the map](./how-to-use-map.md)

### Can I search for facilities in another city?

Yes. Type any city in the search field : the app does not restrict results to your current location.

### Why is the search bar not showing any results?

![No search results screen](/assets/images/home/faq-no-search-results.png)

Most common causes:
- City name spelled incorrectly : check the spelling
- No facilities registered in Spotto in that city
- A too-narrow facility type filter selected : remove the filter and try again
- No internet connection

---

## Sport categories

### What sport categories are available?

Available categories include: Shooting, Squash, Gym, Tennis, Padel, Football, Swimming pool, Badminton, Climbing, and others. The list is dynamic and depends on the facilities in the system. [Full list of categories](./features/categories.md)

### Can I filter facilities by multiple categories at once?

No : currently only one category can be selected at a time. To see facilities from different disciplines, use the search bar without a facility type filter.

---

## Favourites

### How do I add a facility to favourites?

Tap the heart icon (♡) on the facility card in the search results or on the facility details page. [Full guide](./how-to-manage-favorites.md)

![Heart icon on facility card](/assets/images/home/faq-heart-icon.png)

### Do my favourites sync across devices?

Yes. Favourites are linked to your account and sync automatically on all devices where you are logged in.

### How many facilities can I add to favourites?

There is no limit : you can add as many facilities as you like.

---

## Map

### Why is the map not showing my location?

The app needs access to GPS location. Check:
- Whether location is enabled in your phone's settings
- Whether the Spotto app has location permissions
- [How to enable location](./features/map-view.md)

### Can I use the map without location turned on?

Yes, but the map will not show your position or sort facilities by distance. You can manually pan and browse the map.

---

## Booking

### What is the fastest way to book a facility?

The fastest way is the **Quick Book (QuickBook)** feature : one tap on the facility card in the search results. [How to book](./how-to-book-facility.md)

### Can I book a facility for several hours?

Yes. When selecting a time slot, tap multiple adjacent time slots.

### What are "Pending payments"?

These are reservations for which the payment process was not completed. They appear as a notification on the Home screen. Tap the notification to complete the payment.

![Pending payments notification on Home screen](/assets/images/home/faq-pending-payments.png)

### What payment methods are accepted?

Spotto supports payments via TPay:
- Payment cards (Visa, Mastercard)
- Fast bank transfers
- BLIK

![TPay payment methods](/assets/images/home/faq-payment-methods.png)

---

## Community wall

### What is the community wall?

It is a section on the Home screen where users can browse posts, activity, and announcements from the Spotto community. [More about the wall](./features/community-wall.md)

![Community wall section on Home screen](/assets/images/home/faq-community-wall.png)

### Can I turn off the community wall?

The wall is part of the Home screen and cannot be disabled. You can skip it by scrolling past it.

---

## Related topics

- [Home screen : overview](./README.md)
- [Troubleshooting](./troubleshooting.md)

---

# Sport Categories

The sport categories section (Popular Activities) is an interactive carousel on the Home screen that lets you quickly browse venues by sporting discipline.

articles.title: Community Wall
articles.content:
# Community Wall

## Description

The Community Wall is a section on the Home screen where users can browse posts and activity from the Spotto community. This is the place where you can see what is happening in the world of sport in your area.

## What You'll Find on the Wall

![Community wall feed with posts](../../../assets/images/home/feat-community-wall-feed.png)

- **User posts** : information about events, training sessions, and activities
- **Community activity** : what other users are booking and recommending
- **Announcements** : important information from sports venues

## Interactions

From the Community Wall you can:
- **Browse posts** : scroll down to see more content
- **React** : like or comment on a post
- **Share** : share an interesting post
- **Create posts** : tap the button to create a new post

## Creating a Post

1. Tap the **New post** button on the Community Wall
2. Type the content of your post
3. Optionally add a photo or location
4. Tap **Publish**

![Creating a new post on Community Wall](../../../assets/images/home/feat-community-wall-new-post.png)

> 💡 **Tip**: Posts are visible to other Spotto users. Please be mindful of your language and tone.

## Requirements

- You must be logged in to browse and create posts
- Creating posts requires a completed profile

articles.title: Favorites system
articles.content:
# Favorites system

## Description

The favorites system allows you to save sports facilities that you regularly visit. Saved facilities are displayed as cards in the **Favorites** section on the Home screen.

## How to add to favorites

You can add a facility to favorites in two ways:

1. **From search results**: tap the heart icon (♡) on the facility card
2. **From the details page**: tap the heart icon in the upper right corner

Once added, the icon changes to a filled heart (♥).

## Favorites section on the Home screen

The section appears on the Home screen after adding at least one facility. It contains:

- **Facility cards**: mini cards with the name, address, and rating
- **Book button**: quick booking without opening the details
- **Scrolling**: if you have many favorites, scroll the list horizontally

## Managing favorites

- **Removing**: tap the filled heart icon (♥) to remove a facility from favorites
- **Order**: facilities are displayed in the order they were added (newest first)
- **Sync**: favorites are linked to your account and sync across devices

## Requirements

- You must be logged in: favorites do not work without an account
- An internet connection is required for syncing

## Related

- [How to manage favorites: guide](../how-to-manage-favorites.md)
- [How to book a facility](../how-to-book-facility.md)

---

articles.title: Map view
articles.content:
# Map view

## Description

The map view is an interactive section on the Home screen that displays sports facilities in your area. The map uses GPS location to show the nearest places on the map with markers.

## Map elements

| Element | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Blue dot** | Your current location |
| **Colored markers** | Sports facilities (pins on the map) |
| **Crosshair button** | Centers the map on your position |
| **+/- buttons** | Zooming in and out of the map |
| **Info window** | Appears after tapping a marker |

## Navigation gestures

- **Panning**: drag your finger across the map
- **Zooming in**: pinch with two fingers (pinch-to-zoom) or tap +
- **Zooming out**: spread two fingers apart or tap -
- **Double tap**: quick zoom in

## Interacting with markers

After tapping a marker, an info window appears with:
- Facility name
- Address
- Distance from your location
- User rating
- Buttons: **Details** and **Book**

## Location permissions

The map requires access to GPS location. Without it:
- The map still works, but it will not show your position
- Facilities will not be sorted by distance
- The crosshair button will be inactive

### How to enable location

**Android**: Settings → Location → Enable → Spotto → Allow

**iOS**: Settings → Privacy → Location Services → Spotto → While Using

## Related

- [How to use the map: guide](../how-to-use-map.md)
- [Search](./search.md)

---

articles.title: Facility search
articles.content:
# Facility search

## Description

The search tool is the main feature of the Home screen. It is located at the top of the screen and allows you to find sports facilities based on location, date, and facility type.

## Available filters

| Filter | Description | Required |
|--------|-------------|----------|
| **City/location** | City name or address | Yes |
| **Date** | Booking day (default: today) | No |
| **Facility type** | Type of facility (court, shooting range, gym...) | No |

## How the search works

1. **Autocomplete**: as you type a city name, you will see suggestions. Tap a suggestion to select it.
2. **Search**: after filling in the filters, tap the search button or confirm with the keyboard.
3. **Results**: a list of facilities sorted by relevance and distance.

## Result card

Each search result is displayed as a card with the following information:

- **Facility name**: the official name of the place
- **Address**: street, number, city
- **Type**: type of facility (e.g., "Squash court")
- **Availability**: available time slots for the selected day
- **Price**: approximate price per hour
- **Rating**: average user rating (stars)
- **Book button**: quick access to booking

## Sorting results

Results are sorted by relevance by default. Factors that affect the order:
- Match to the query
- Distance from the user's location
- Facility popularity
- Time slot availability

## Limitations

- The search requires an internet connection
- Results are limited to facilities registered in the Spotto system
- Autocomplete works for Polish cities

## Related

- [How to search for a facility: guide](../how-to-search-facilities.md)
- [How to book a facility](../how-to-book-facility.md)
